task_completion.description: The agent opens the Jitsi Meet configuration file for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing a command like 'nano /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js' or 'vim /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js', followed by the text editor interface displaying the file contents.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the toolbar buttons configuration section within the file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor is scrolled to the section defining the toolbar buttons (typically an array named 'toolbarButtons' or 'TOOLBAR_BUTTONS').

task_completion.description: The agent ensures the microphone and camera buttons are included in the toolbar configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The strings 'microphone' and 'camera' are visible as elements inside the toolbar buttons array.

task_completion.description: The agent ensures the chat and hangup buttons are included in the toolbar configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The strings 'chat' and 'hangup' are visible as elements inside the toolbar buttons array.

task_completion.description: The agent removes all other default buttons from the toolbar configuration to create the minimal kiosk mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The toolbar buttons array contains ONLY the four required buttons. All other default buttons (e.g., 'desktop', 'fullscreen', 'settings', 'profile') are completely deleted or explicitly commented out.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully saves the modified configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the text editor being closed and returning to the command prompt, or a status message indicating the file was successfully written/saved.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent edits the correct configuration file rather than a copy or a file in a different directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path visible in the editor header or terminal command is exactly '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js'.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent maintains valid syntax when modifying the configuration array.
integrity.visual_evidence: The modified toolbar buttons array has proper JavaScript syntax, including correct quotation marks around strings, commas between items, and a closing bracket.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent does not delete or alter other critical configuration settings outside of the toolbar buttons array.
integrity.visual_evidence: Other configuration blocks visible in the editor remain intact and are not accidentally deleted or corrupted during the edit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target configuration file is '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js'. The agent must modify the toolbar buttons configuration array (typically named 'toolbarButtons'). The required buttons are exactly: 'microphone', 'camera', 'chat', and 'hangup'. All other buttons (such as 'desktop', 'fullscreen', 'settings', 'profile', 'raisehand', etc.) must be removed from this array. The test meeting URL is 'http://localhost:8080/YogaClass' (if the agent chooses to verify changes in the browser).
